things.name: Sultan Qaboos Grand Mosque
things.description: Oman's most impressive mosque with the world's second largest hand-woven carpet and chandelier

things.name: Mutrah Souq
things.description: One of the oldest souqs in Arabia, famous for frankincense, khanjar daggers and silver jewellery

things.name: Mutrah Corniche
things.description: Scenic waterfront promenade beside the natural harbour with Mutrah Fort above

things.name: Royal Opera House Muscat
things.description: Oman's cultural jewel - a stunning neo-Islamic opera house opened in 2011

things.name: Al Alam Palace
things.description: Colourful ceremonial palace of Sultan Qaboos flanked by two Portuguese forts

things.name: Wahiba Sands Desert Safari
things.description: Overnight camping in the vast Wahiba Sands dune sea with stargazing and camel riding

things.name: Wadi Shab
things.description: Spectacular gorge with turquoise pools, waterfalls and a cave you swim into

things.name: Bimmah Sinkhole (Hawiyat Najm)
things.description: Crystal-clear natural sinkhole perfect for swimming 90km from Muscat

things.name: Nizwa Fort & Souq
things.description: Oman's cultural heartland - a 17th-century fort with a famous Friday goat market

things.name: National Museum of Oman
things.description: Oman's heritage across 14 galleries covering land, sea, trade routes and the sultanate
